title: Relocate QMS Manual to standalone repo at Quality-Manual/

comment:
PRE-REVIEW: COMPLIANT. Detailed findings follow.

FRONTMATTER:
- title: 'Relocate QMS Manual to standalone repo at Quality-Manual/' -- present, non-empty, descriptive
- revision_summary: 'Initial draft' -- present, appropriate for v0.1

PLACEHOLDER CHECK:
- No {{double-curly}} author-time placeholders remaining
- Execution-time [SQUARE_BRACKETS] correctly present in Sections 10-12
- No TBD, TODO, FIXME, or XXX found

DOCUMENT STRUCTURE (all 12 required sections present):
1. Purpose, 2. Scope (2.1-2.3), 3. Current State, 4. Proposed State, 5. Change Description (5.1-5.4), 6. Justification, 7. Impact Assessment (7.1-7.5), 8. Testing Summary (Automated + Integration), 9. Implementation Plan (9.1-9.8), 10. Execution (EI table + Comments), 11. Execution Summary, 12. References

SCOPE: Well-defined. Six discrete change items clearly enumerated with specific files listed in Section 2.3.

EI TRACEABILITY: All 18 EIs trace to implementation plan phases. No plan items are missing from the EI table. EI-1 and EI-18 correctly bracket execution per SOP-004 Section 5.

IMPACT ASSESSMENT: Section 7.1 provides a detailed files-affected table. Section 7.2 identifies four SDLC documents. Section 7.3 correctly identifies downstream impact on existing projects.

DEVELOPMENT CONTROLS (Section 7.4): All 7 required controls specified -- test environment isolation, branch isolation, write protection, qualification, CI verification, PR gate, submodule update.

QUALIFIED STATE CONTINUITY (Section 7.5): Populated with concrete values (f6f82db, v21.0/v26.0, CLI-17.0). Not placeholders.

TESTING SUMMARY: Automated verification specifies test suite with expected count (687). Integration verification provides three concrete verification steps (clone check, submodule population, stale reference grep).

VR COLUMN: All blank. Acceptable -- this is a repository restructuring CR with no behavioral verification required (per QMS-Policy Section 4.3).

MERGE GATE: Phase 7 prerequisite correctly states RS and RTM must be EFFECTIVE before merging.

FORMATTING: Consistent heading levels, table formatting, and markdown syntax throughout.

REFERENCES: SOP-001, SOP-002, SOP-005, SOP-006, CR-103, CR-104 -- all relevant.

REVIEWER ASSIGNMENT: No TU assignment needed. This CR modifies no application code -- it restructures repository layout, updates path references, and revises SDLC documents. None of the available domain TUs (tu_ui, tu_scene, tu_sketch, tu_sim) have relevant expertise. BU not needed (no user-facing behavioral change). QA review alone is sufficient per Quality Unit Handbook guidance on template/process changes.
